title: Bittersweet Creamy Irish Beef Stew
description: A rich, savory stew featuring tender beef, caramelized onions, and a splash of brandy, finished with a velvety cream sauce. Nostalgic Irish flavors wrapped in a bittersweet, high-protein comfort dish.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat 1 tbsp of butter over medium-high heat and brown the beef cubes in batches, then set aside.
Add the remaining butter and the diced onion to the pot and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return the beef to the pot and pour in the brandy, allowing it to deglaze and simmer for 2 minutes, scraping up any browned bits.
Add the carrots, parsnips, beef broth, thyme, rosemary, and bay leaf, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low.
Cover the pot and simmer the stew for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beef is tender.
Remove the bay leaf, stir in the heavy cream, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Simmer for an additional 10 minutes until the sauce thickens to a silky consistency.
Serve hot over mashed potatoes or crusty bread, garnishing with a sprinkle of fresh parsley if desired.
